activity_results.name: Connected
stages.name: Inquiry
activities.notes: Intake call transcript. Caller: Claire, daughter. She explained that her mother Margaret fell in the kitchen two weeks ago and has been unsteady since. Margaret is living alone in a two-story home in Naperville and is managing her own medications, though Claire has found doses missed. The family is looking for four mornings a week of help with bathing, dressing, and breakfast preparation, ideally starting within the month. Claire asked about caregiver consistency and whether the same person would come each visit. We discussed our matching process and the free in-home assessment. Claire will speak with her brother Trevor this weekend and confirm a time for the visit. She also asked whether long-term care insurance is accepted; advised she send the policy details so we can review benefits before the assessment.

activity_types.name: Note
stages.name: Start of Care
activities.notes: Care plan summary ahead of start of care. Walter is a widower living alone in Lisle since his wife passed in 2024. He is independent with transfers but needs standby assistance for showering and has had two near-falls getting out of the tub. His daughter Adaeze is a physician in Chicago and visits on weekends; she is the decision maker and prefers written updates after each visit. Walter is a Korean War veteran and the family is pursuing an Aid and Attendance benefit, which may change the payer mix later. Preferences noted: he is a private person, dislikes small talk in the morning, enjoys discussing baseball, and wants caregivers to arrive at 8am sharp. No pets. Kitchen access is fine; he cooks his own dinner and does not want that taken over.
